+/* Click indicator — scale up & fade out */

   transform: translateY(0);

+/* Dark mode — prefers-color-scheme */
+@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {

+    background: rgba(64, 64, 64, 0.9);
+    border-color: transparent;
+    color: #e4e4e7;

+  }

+
+/* Dark mode — .dark class on ancestor */
+.dark .rr-video-controls,
+:root.dark .rr-video-controls {
+  background: rgba(64, 64, 64, 0.9);
+  border-color: transparent;
+  color: #e4e4e7;
+}
+
+.dark .rr-video-progress-track,
+:root.dark .rr-video-progress-track {

+}

+}

+

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-02-11 09:25:54Z)**_

Skill usage: no specialized skill applied; this request is a general security/maintainability diff review rather than a skill-specific workflow.

1. [HIGH] Cross-origin download regression  
File: `packages/rich-renderer-video/src/VideoRenderer.tsx:156`  
Issue: `fetch(src)` requires CORS permission. The previous anchor-based approach could still download many cross-origin video URLs; this one can fail silently on common CDN/media hosts without CORS headers.  
Fix: Use anchor-based download as primary/fallback for cross-origin URLs, or explicitly detect CORS failure and fallback to direct navigation/download.

2. [HIGH] Large video download can exhaust browser memory  
File: `packages/rich-renderer-video/src/VideoRenderer.tsx:157`  
Issue: `res.blob()` buffers the full video in memory before triggering download. Large files can freeze or crash the tab.  
Fix: Prefer browser-native direct download (`<a href=...>`) or a streaming approach; avoid full in-memory buffering for large media.

3. [MEDIUM] Missing HTTP/network error handling in download flow  
File: `packages/rich-renderer-video/src/VideoRenderer.tsx:156`  
Issue: No `res.ok` check and no `catch`; 4xx/5xx responses and network failures are not handled, and users get no actionable feedback.  
Fix: Check `res.ok`, add `catch`, expose an error state/toast, and optionally report failures to telemetry.

4. [MEDIUM] Rapid clicks can trigger duplicate downloads  
File: `packages/rich-renderer-video/src/VideoRenderer.tsx:154`  
Issue: `isDownloading` is state-based and only blocks after rerender; button is not disabled, so very fast repeated clicks can start multiple requests.  
Fix: Add `disabled={isDownloading}` on the button and an in-flight ref lock (or `AbortController`) in `downloadVideo`.

5. [LOW] Object URL is revoked immediately after click  
File: `packages/rich-renderer-video/src/VideoRenderer.tsx:164`  
Issue: Immediate `URL.revokeObjectURL(url)` can race with download start in some browsers.  
Fix: Revoke on a delayed tick (`setTimeout`) after click.

6. [LOW] Keyboard focus visibility is not explicitly styled  
File: `packages/rich-renderer-video/src/styles.css:126`  
Issue: Controls define hover/active animation but no explicit `:focus-visible` style, which can reduce keyboard accessibility clarity.  
Fix: Add `:focus-visible` outline/box-shadow styles for `.rr-video-btn` and slider thumb.

7. [LOW] Missing tests for new download/loading behavior  
File: `packages/rich-renderer-video/src/VideoRenderer.tsx:153`  
Issue: No tests covering download success/failure, CORS failure fallback, spinner state, and rapid-click guard.  
Fix: Add component tests for these paths.

Verdict: BLOCK
